General safety information
Important safety instructions
1.1 Important safety instructions
It is important to install, operate, maintain, and dispose of the equipment according to the
information in the product documentation, and in compliance with local regulations. For the
safety of the users and the patients, before a user operates or works on the equipment, they
must read, understand, and obey all:
• Warnings, cautions, instructions, and notes in this document and the related
documents.
• Safety instructions, emergency procedures, and information in this document and the
related documents.
• Warnings, cautions, and safety markings on the equipment and the accessories of the
equipment.
If you have local work instructions for the equipment, then Elekta recommends that you include
the warnings, cautions, and notes from this document in your local work instructions.
1.1.1 Conventions for warnings, cautions, and notes
The conventions give information about the class and the level of the risk in the warnings and
cautions, and the content of notes.
WARNING 1.1
A warning is information about procedures, tasks, or conditions with a risk of fatal or serious
injury, injury, or clinical mistreatment, if you do not obey the instructions.
CAUTION 1.1
A caution is information about procedures, tasks, or conditions with a risk of damage to the
equipment, data, or the environment, if you do not obey the instructions.
Note: A note gives more information about a related text.
1.1.2 Expected service life of the equipment
The expected service life is the length of time that the regular service and repair procedures can
keep the equipment safe to operate.
WARNING 1.2
Do not operate the equipment unless you correctly complete the safety checks, planned
maintenance schedules, and extended life checks. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
The expected service life of this equipment is 15 years of normal use.
The Elekta definition for 'expected service life' is the length of time that the regular service and
repair procedures can keep the equipment safe to operate.
The Elekta definition of 'normal use' is the treatment of 50 patients each day for five days each
week of the year. If the equipment is used more frequently than this, the frequency of the
planned maintenance must increase accordingly and the extended life checks must also be
brought forward accordingly.

General safety information
Important safety instructions
The level of operation of the equipment can be different to ‘normal use’. This has an effect on the
length of the service life. Because of wear of parts, the risk of a malfunction can increase after 10
years operation. Elekta recommends that before you continue operation after 10 years, you
complete the regular extended life checks.
Elekta can help the User with the correct safety checks, planned maintenance schedules, and the
regular extended life checks for continued safe operation of the equipment.
The system must be maintained throughout its expected service life in compliance with the EMC
information detailed in the applicable Instructions for Use.
For information on the above checks and schedules, contact your local Elekta representative.

1.2 Safety precautions
1.2.1 Safety precautions for the compatibility of the equipment
WARNING 1.3
Do not use parts, accessories, or other equipment, without approval by Elekta of their
compatibility. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury and damage to the
equipment.
Only operate the equipment with Elekta supplied or approved, compatible equipment or parts.
Accessories, transducers, and cables that are not specified by or do not have the approval of
Elekta can cause an incorrect treatment delivery. They can also have an effect on the performance
of the electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), which can increase the emissions or decrease the
immunity of the equipment.
Contact Elekta for information about the compatibility of other equipment or parts.
1.2.2 Safety precautions for the electromagnetic compatibility
The design and manufacture of Elekta products is in compliance with applicable EMC standards.
The intended result of the standards is to minimize the risk of electromagnetic interference by RF
devices. The type of device and the distance to the equipment has an effect on the intensity of
the electromagnetic interference. The equipment includes the linear accelerator and accessories,
the control cabinets, the computers in the control room, and equipment from other
manufacturer.

General safety information
Safety precautions
It is necessary to have special precautions for EMC emissions. Elekta recommends that you obey
these precautions when you use RF devices near the equipment.
• Do not use RF devices unless they are in compliance with international standards for
electromagnetic compatibility, for example, the CE or FCC mark.
• Do not use a two-way emergency radio in the treatment room or the control room.
Only use an emergency radio if there is an emergency.
• Do not use cellular telephones, or other devices with cellular modems near the
equipment. The Instructions for Use contains information on the minimum safe
distance of these devices from the equipment.
• When you use a device that is enabled for wireless operation, it must be a minimum of
10 cm from the equipment.
• Radio-Frequency Identification (RFID) readers, when activated to do a scan of a RFID
tag, must be a minimum of one meter from the equipment.
Note: The RFID systems that Elekta supply use lower power levels. You can safely use these RFID systems
when you obey the procedures in the Instructions for Use for the product.
The system must be installed, put into service, and maintained in compliance with the EMC
information in the Instructions for Use.
Contact Elekta for information about the compatibility of parts and equipment.
WARNING 1.4
Do not put portable or mobile radio frequency (RF) communication devices near to the
equipment. The emissions from such RF devices can be more than the EMC standards, and can
have unwanted effects on the equipment operation. If you ignore this warning, the RF
emissions can cause the equipment to have a malfunction. A malfunction can cause fatal injury,
clinical mistreatment, or damage to the equipment.
WARNING 1.5
Do not use accessories, transducers, and cables that are not specified by Elekta. They can have
an effect on the EMC performance, which can increase EMC emissions or decrease the
immunity to RF interference of the equipment. If you ignore this warning, incorrect radiation
delivery and other malfunctions can occur, which can cause fatal injury or clinical mistreatment.
1.2.3 Safety precaution for Service Tools and lifting equipment
WARNING 1.6
Do not use defective or damaged lifting equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
WARNING 1.7
Do not use incorrectly calibrated or defective Service Tools. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause injury and damage to the equipment.
Before you use the Service Tool or lifting equipment, make sure that:
• There are no defective or damaged parts
• The calibration of the Service Tool is correct

Safety precautions

For information on the maintenance and calibration schedules of the Service Tool, see the

1.2.4 Safety precaution for work with electrical and mechanical parts
WARNING 1.8
Do not connect any electrical power supply before the ground earth connections are secure.
There is a risk of high leakage current. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.9
Do not remove the cables or the covers, or operate the equipment without the covers. If you
ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury
The covers on the equipment prevent access to high voltage parts and parts that move. If you
remove the covers, there is a risk of high voltage shock or injury from parts that move.
Only remove the covers if you are an Elekta approved person with the correct authority and you
obey the applicable safety procedures.
1.2.5 Safety precaution for work with safety devices during service
operation
WARNING 1.10
Do not remove, change, or override the safety switches, interlocks, and other safety devices. If
you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury or clinical mistreatment.
The procedures in this document can tell you to remove, change, or override the safety switches,
interlocks, or other safety devices during repair or maintenance work. When the work is
completed, make all the safety devices active again, and do a test of them.
1.2.6 Safety precaution for work on BLDs with UV lighting
WARNING 1.11
Do not look into the ultraviolet lighting of the BLD without protective glasses. If you ignore this
warning, you can cause serious injury.
The covers and screens of the BLD prevent an injury to your eyes from the ultraviolet lighting.
Before you remove the covers or screens of the BLD, switch off the linear accelerator, or use
protective glasses. Protective glasses must have the UV-A specification OD 6+ @ 190 - 532 Nm.
If there is damage to the covers or screen of the BLD, switch off the linear accelerator and contact
the Elekta representative.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.7 Safety precaution for work with lasers
WARNING 1.12
Do not look into the laser beams because they can cause damage to your eyes. If you ignore
this warning, you can cause serious injury.
Elekta systems use the laser beams to help set up patients in the correct position for treatment.
During your work in the treatment room, make sure that you do not look into the laser beams.
1.2.8 Safety precautions for work with display screen equipment
WARNING 1.13
Do not use the display screen equipment for long periods of time. If you ignore this warning,
you can cause injury.
When you use the display screen equipment (DSE), obey the applicable Health and Safety
regulations and procedures in the jurisdiction where you do the work.
1.2.9 Safety precautions for cleaning and disinfection
WARNING 1.14
Do not clean the equipment, or use a disinfectant spray when the equipment is connected to
the electrical supply. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.15
Do not use a spray that is explosive or flammable. If you ignore this warning, you can cause
fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
CAUTION 1.2
Do not let water and other liquids go into the equipment. If you ignore this caution, you can
cause damage to the equipment.
CAUTION 1.3
Do not use a spray to clean the medical equipment room. If you ignore this caution, you can
cause damage to the equipment.
At regular intervals, it is necessary to clean the equipment and equipment room with
disinfectants. Elekta recommends that the procedures to clean the equipment and the equipment
room obey the applicable laws and regulations of the jurisdiction where the equipment is
installed.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.10 Safety precautions for work with high temperatures on the BLD
covers
During normal operation, the maximum temperatures of the accessory ring and BLD outer covers
can increase to 45 °C. Make sure that you do not touch the outer covers or the accessory ring for
more than 1 minute.
1.2.11 Safety precaution for the installation of accessories on the BLD
WARNING 1.16
Do not touch the connector found in the accessory ring on the BLD. If you ignore this warning,
you can cause injury.
When you install an accessory, for example, a shadow tray, electron applicator, or front pointer,
do not touch the pins in the connector. The pins have a low electrical current, which can give an
electric shock when you touch them.
1.2.12 Safety precautions for work with heavy parts
The safety precautions for work with heavy parts are the necessary safety precautions to know to
prevent injury.
WARNING 1.17
Do not lift or move heavy parts unless you obey the applicable Health and Safety regulations
and procedures. If you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.
1.2.13 Safety precautions during work on the BLD
When you do work on the BLD, make sure that you obey the following safety precautions:
WARNING 1.18
Do not do work on the inner parts of the beam limiting device while the electrical supply is
connected. There is a risk of high voltage electric shock. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.19
Do not do work on the beam limiting device unless the gantry is at 0° or 180°. Gravity can cause
the leaves, diaphragms, and dynamic leaf guides to move. If you ignore this warning, you can
cause fatal injury.
WARNING 1.20
Do not touch the hot LED field light projector, or the heat sink, immediately after operation. If
you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.14 Safety precaution for the removal of parts from the radiation head
WARNING 1.21
Do not touch the flight tube, bending magnet, or other parts near to them until a check for
induced radioactivity is completed. Excessive exposure to radioactivity is dangerous. If you
ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury.
After the operation of the linear accelerator at high energy, the parts in the radiation head can
become radioactive and a radiation hazard. Before you do work on, or remove parts from the
radiation head, use a calibrated radiation detector to measure the level of induced radioactivity.
Use the applicable safety procedures when you remove radioactive parts and material. The
maximum dose rate for the safe removal of radioactive parts and material is 50 µSv/hr
(microsievert per hour). Do not remove the radioactive parts and material from the treatment
room before they are correctly prepared for safe removal.
1.2.15 Safety precautions for maintenance, modification, and
decommissioning
WARNING 1.22
Do not do work, or make modifications to the equipment without the correct training and
authority. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury, clinical mistreatment, and
damage to the equipment.
Only do work on, or make modifications to, the equipment if you are an Elekta approved person
with the applicable authority. Such work must use best practice and obey the laws and
regulations in the jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed.
Modifications or work that does not have approval by Elekta, and is not done by an Elekta
approved person with the correct authority, can cause:
• Fatal injury
• Incorrect radiation delivery
• Malfunction of the equipment
• Damage to the equipment
• The warranty to be canceled.
1.2.16 Safety precautions for work in areas of induced radioactivity
The safety precautions for work in areas of induced radioactivity are the precautions to prevent
an excessive exposure to, and serious injury from, induced radioactivity.
When you operate or do work on the equipment, Elekta recommends that you use the applicable
radiation protection functions, devices, systems, procedures, and accessories. Always obey the
applicable laws and regulations for induced radioactivity for the jurisdiction in which the
equipment is installed.
The safety precautions are applicable to:
• Maintenance, modifications, and decommissioning
• Measurement of the induced radioactivity
• Removal and movement of parts with induced radioactivity.

General safety information
Safety precautions
1.2.16.1 Safety precautions for the measurement of induced radioactivity
The safety precautions for the measurement of induced radioactivity are the necessary safety
precautions to know to prevent excessive exposure to radioactivity, which can cause serious
injury or damage to the environment.
Always use two radiation detectors to measure the level of induced radioactivity. Only use
radiation detectors that have an applicable calibration certificate. The period of time since the
last calibration date must be less than 12 months. Do not use the radiation detectors if the period
of time since the last calibration date is more than 12 months.
WARNING 1.23
Do not move radioactive material (by air, sea, or land) unless you obey the applicable laws and
regulations for induced radioactivity and disposal of radioactive material of the jurisdictions
through which the movement occurs. If you ignore this warning, you can cause serious injury
and damage to the environment.
1.2.16.2 Safety precautions for handling parts with induced radioactivity
The handling of parts with induced radioactivity must comply with the laws and regulations of the
jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed. The safe levels of radiation applicable in the UK
do not apply in all jurisdictions. The permitted level of radioactivity must always be as low as
reasonably achievable (ALARA). This level may vary depending on local rules, therefore the safe
working levels from the applicable local rules must be considered as the acceptable working limit.
If there are no safe levels available for a jurisdiction, use the UK value as a guideline, which is a
maximum of 150 cps at the surface for a maximum duration of 72 minutes. The following table
shows the conversion from this value to other units.
Table 1.2 Conversion between the units of induced radioactivity
Counts per second Microsieverts per Milliroentgen per
(cps) hour (µSv/hr) hour (mR/hr)
150 50 5
For information on the safe removal of parts with induced radioactivity and the safe levels of
radiation for your jurisdiction, contact Elekta or a Qualified Expert (QE).
1.3 Emergencies
1.3.1 Safety precaution for the occurrence of fire or explosion
WARNING 1.24
Do not use the equipment in rooms that contain explosive or flammable gases. If you ignore
this warning, you can cause fatal injury and damage to the equipment.
High levels of oxygen, and some anesthetic gases and disinfectant sprays, can make an explosive
or flammable environment in the room.

General safety information
Emergencies
1.3.2 Recommendations for emergency procedures
Before you use the equipment, Elekta recommends that you know all the local emergency
procedures and the locations of all the emergency switches.
The Elekta equipment includes:
• The STOP MOTORS buttons on the handheld controllers and user interface panels in
the treatment room, and FKP in the control room.
When you press these buttons, it disconnects the electrical supply from the motors,
stops all movements of the equipment, and stops kV and MV radiation.
• The EMERGENCY POWER OFF buttons in the treatment room, control area, and areas
near the equipment.
When you operate these buttons, it disconnects the electrical supply to the linear
accelerator. The buttons disconnect the electrical supply from many electrical circuits,
but not from all the circuits.

2.1.2 Recommendations for training
The regulations for training give a description of the necessary training, regulations, and
compliance that apply to the operation of the equipment.
Different countries have different regulations for training. Make sure that you have the necessary
training before you operate or do work on the equipment. Make sure that your training is in
compliance with the laws and regulations of the jurisdiction in which the equipment is installed.
Training is available from Elekta. Contact Elekta for more information about the applicable
training courses.
2.1.3 Disposal of the equipment
End of life (EOL) disposal is the procedure to disassemble, remove, and discard the equipment, in
full or in parts, to a condition where it is not possible to operate the equipment for its intended
use.
Only a Qualified Expert (QE) and an approved facility with an applicable license, may remove and
discard the equipment and recycle the material.
A full description of disposal requirements is detailed in a separate document Product Disposal
Information. This document is available on request from Elekta.
The customer owns the data on the storage media. If there are any data storage media such as
HDDs, remove them from the device and hand them to the customer for disposal. If the customer
entrusts the disposal team to dispose of the data, the data must be destroyed in accordance with
relevant cybersecurity regulations.

CAUTION 2.2
Do not touch the parts identified with the ESD caution label. Always obey the ESD precautions
during work on these parts. If you ignore this caution, you can cause damage to the equipment.
Semiconductor devices, integrated circuits, and the parts that contain them, are ESD sensitive
devices. These devices are electrostatic discharge sensitive (ESDS) devices. ESD can cause damage
to these devices and cause them to have a malfunction immediately or some time after the
electrostatic discharge. To prevent the ESD damage to these devices, always wear a grounded
antistatic wrist strap, and obey the applicable ESD precautions and procedures.
Elekta recommends that all users receive the training about ESD. The training must include the
ESD caution label and these ESD precautions.

Introduction
Document scope
3.1 Document scope
The corrective maintenance manual (CMM) helps service engineers troubleshoot, repair and
maintain the systems, subsystems and components of the linear accelerator. The target audience
is qualified service engineers. These may be engineers employed directly by Elekta, qualified
representatives of Elekta or qualified representatives of Elekta's customers.
The manual provides:
• A description of the system or sub-system in question.
• A troubleshooting section to identify the problem areas.
• A set of detailed procedures used to replace FRUs (field replaceable units).
• A section on how to return the device to work.
If you find any mistakes in this document, or identify areas for improvement, please raise a CLM
(customer lifecycle management) case and assign it to the relevant queue.

Cover sets for the Elekta linear accelerator
4.1 Cover sets for the Elekta linear accelerator
All Elekta linear accelerators are installed with a set of covers, which give:
• Protection to patients and users from hazardous parts and conditions in the gantry arm
and beam limiting device (BLD).
• Protection against damage during treatment to the parts of the linear accelerator in
the treatment room.
• A comfortable environment for patients during treatment.
The gantry arm and BLD of the linear accelerator have covers installed around their structures.
There are no covers on the openings for the treatment field.
Some covers attach to posts of the frame installed between the treatment room and the
equipment room. Patients and users in the treatment room cannot see the internal structure of
the linear accelerator.
In most conditions, the covers for the linear accelerator do not make a full partition wall between
the treatment room and the equipment room. It is usual to add more panels to extend the wall
across the full width of the treatment room in the A-B direction. These panels usually include one
or two access doors to the equipment room.
Different cover sets have different assemblies and are different colors.

4.3.1 Description of the covers on the beam limiting device
The cover set of the Agility™, MLCi2™ and Beam Modulator BLDs have two semicircular covers,
one on the left side and one on the right side of the BLD.
The MLCi2™ BLD cover has an opening on the side for the BLD control unit. On the MLCi2™, a
small rubber panel gives protection to the openings for the BLD control units. This rubber panel is
part of the BLD cover. On the Beam Modulator™, the BLD control unit does not have a protective
rubber panel.
All the BLD covers have a semicircular scale which, when put together, gives a 360° rotation scale
for the BLD. The two panels are not the same, because the scale of the right panel is different to
that of the left panel.
All three BLD covers are not connected to and have no contact with the touchguard ring.
4.3.2 Description of the inner arm covers
These systems have two inner arm covers. One inner arm cover is installed on the left side of the
gantry arm, and one on the right side. The shape of the covers is a mirror image of each other.
The left inner arm cover has a pointer on the BLD scale.
Screws attach each inner arm cover to the outer arm cover. Self-adhesive seals attach the front
and center of each inner arm cover to each other.
4.3.3 Description of the outer arm covers
These systems have one outer arm installed on the top section of the gantry arm. Two brackets
and four nuts attach the G-end of the outer arm cover to the gantry arm.
4.3.4 Description of the gantry drum cover
These systems have a circular gantry drum cover. Screws attach the gantry drum cover directly to
the gantry drum.
A gantry scale assembly attaches to the center hole in the gantry cover. The other end of the
cover has a shape cut in it for the iViewGT™ detector arm and an XVI imaging system.
4.3.5 Description of the ring covers of the gantry drum
These systems have two semicircular ring covers. One ring cover installs on the left side of the
gantry drum cover, and one on the right side. Four screws attach the ring covers to the gantry
drum. The ring covers do not go across the full 360° range of the gantry drum.
One end of each ring cover has a shape cut in it for the gantry arm. The other end of the ring
cover has a shape cut in it for the iViewGT™ detector arm.

4.3.6 Description of the center disc
The center disc is installed across the hole in the center of the gantry drum cover. Eight screws
install through the holes in the gantry drum cover to hold the center disc in position.
The center disc can have an optional gantry scale.
4.3.7 Description of the lower quadrant fascia panels
Four screws and a packing piece attach the left and right lower quadrant fascia panels together.
Screws attach the fascia panels to the fascia support posts between the treatment room and the
equipment room.
Because the fascia panels are large and flexible, they can bend during installation. Velcro® pads
are installed at the center of each panel to help prevent this.
4.3.8 Description of the upper quadrant fascia panels
Four screws and a packing piece attach the left and right upper quadrant fascia panels together.
Screws attach the panels to the fascia support posts between the treatment room and the
equipment room.
4.3.9 Description of the treatment room monitors
These systems have two TRMs, one in each upper quadrant fascia panel. The TRMs attach to a
bracket on the fascia support posts between the treatment room and the equipment room. They
are not attached to the upper quadrant fascia panels.
The SVGA cable from the TCC connects to the TRM behind the partition wall. Some earlier
configurations have an isolation transformer installed on the bracket which is the power supply
for the TRMs.
4.3.10 Description of TRM bezels and handset rails
Each TRM has a bezel around the front of the TRM. The bezel attaches to the upper fascia
quadrant panel. A bracket attached to the bezels supplies an installation point for the HHC
receiver unit.
The handset rails for the HHCs attach to the TRM bezels. The HHC cable, and the ground (earth)
cable of the handset rail go through the holes in the bezel. The HHC cable connects to the HHC
receiver unit. The ground (earth) cable connects to the bracket of the bezel.
If the linear accelerator is installed in a smaller width treatment room, the cover set does not
have sufficient space for TRMs and HHCs. Therefore, the TRMs bezels and handset rails are not
used.
4.3.11 Description of the narrow covers
The narrow covers have a smaller width than the standard cover set. It is almost the same as the
standard cover set but does not have sufficient space for TRMs and HHCs. Therefore, the upper
quadrant fascia panels do not have the holes for TRMs, bezels or handset rails.

6.1.2 Repair and replacement of the covers on the BLD
6.1.2.1 Removing the covers on an asymmetric diaphragm or MLCi™, or MLCi2™ BLD
WARNING 6.1
Do not touch electrical parts, or parts that move, after you remove the covers or cables of the
equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
Note: The covers on the equipment prevent access to high voltage parts, and parts that move. After you
remove the covers, there is a risk of high voltage shock or injury from parts that move. Always
install the covers before you put the equipment into operation again.
1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Rotate the BLD to 0°.
WARNING 6.2
Do not do work on the equipment before you disconnect the electrical supply to the work area
to prevent a high voltage electric shock. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
3 Disconnect the electrical supply from the linear accelerator.
4 Remove the two M6 × 60 mm screws on the touchguard ring and remove the touchguard
ring.
5 Remove the black knob on each of the thumbwheels on the BLD.
6 Remove the seven M4 × 8 mm screws from the BLD rotating cover.
7 Remove the BLD rotating cover.
8 At the lower collimator cover, touchguard and pointer assembly, remove the M3 × 16 mm
fasteners on the access panel to the microswitches.
This gives access to the microswitches for the touchguard ring.
9 Disconnect the cable from the microswitches.
10 Remove the two access panels.
11 Remove the two M4 × 16 mm screws, and remove the lower collimator cover, touchguard,
and pointer assembly.
12 Move the lower collimator cover away from the BLD and work area.
13 At the G-end of the collimator cover, disengage the bracket that attaches the lower
collimator cover to the collimator cover.
14 Route the touchguard cable through the cut-out until the cable is free.

6.1.3.1 Removing the inner arm cover
WARNING 6.5
Do not lift the inner arm cover without the correct lifting equipment. The inner arm cover is
heavy, and more than one person is necessary to lift or move it. If you ignore this warning, you
can cause serious injury and damage to the equipment.
WARNING 6.6
Do not touch electrical parts, or parts that move, after you remove the covers or cables of the
equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Remove the BLD covers.
3 Disconnect the linear accelerator from the electrical supply.
4 Remove the two M6 screws that attach the inner arm cover to the outer arm cover.
5 Carefully disengage the four pins on the inner arm cover from the holes in the outer arm
cover.
6 Carefully lift and move the inner arm cover away from of the work area.

6.1.3.2 Installing the inner arm cover
WARNING 6.7
Do not lift the inner arm cover without the correct equipment to lift heavy equipment. The
inner arm cover is heavy, and more than one person is necessary to lift or move it. If you ignore
this warning, it can cause serious injury and damage to the equipment.
Note: Install the outer arm cover before the inner arm cover.
1 Rotate the gantry to 180°.
2 Rotate the BLD to 0°.
3 Disconnect the linear accelerator from the electrical supply.
4 Move the inner arm cover into position, and align the four pins on the cover with the holes
on the outer arm cover.
5 If the four pins do not align with the holes on the outer arm cover:
a Remove the inner arm cover.
b Adjust the flange nuts on the outer arm cover.
c Do step 5 again until the covers align correctly.
6 Use two M6 × 20 mm screws and attach the inner arm cover to the outer arm cover.

Service parts repair and replacement
Repair and replacement of the legacy quadrant covers
WARNING 6.11
Do not touch electrical parts, or parts that move, after you remove the covers or cables of the
equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
Note: The covers on the equipment prevent access to high voltage parts, and parts that move. After you
remove the covers, there is a risk of high voltage shock or injury from parts that move. Always
install the covers before you put the equipment into operation again.
1 Rotate the gantry to 0°.
2 Remove the two M8 × 65 mm screws and washers that attach the upper quadrant fascia
panel to the fascia support post.
3 Carefully move the panels from behind the gantry drum cover, and then remove the panels.
4 Move the panel away from the work area.
5 Remove the M8 × 25 mm screws that attach the upper quadrant fascia panels together. If
installed, remove the seal between the two covers.
6.1.5.2 Installing the upper quadrant fascia panels
WARNING 6.12
Do not touch electrical parts, or parts that move, after you remove the covers or cables of the
equipment. If you ignore this warning, you can cause fatal injury.
Note: The covers on the equipment prevent access to high voltage parts, and parts that move. After you
remove the covers, there is a risk of high voltage shock or injury from parts that move. Always
install the covers before you put the equipment into operation again.
1 Rotate the gantry to 0°.
2 Put the front surface of the left and right upper quadrant fascia panels on a clean, flat
surface. If it is necessary, put the front surfaces vertically against a clean, flat wall.
3 Put a sheet of paper temporarily on the Velcro® pads on the rear surfaces of the fascia
panels.
4 If there is a Velcro® pad on the bolts on the gantry drum, put a piece of paper on the
surface of the pads.
5 Put a 6 mm packing piece between the panels. Make sure that the holes align.
6 Use two M8 × 25 mm screws to attach the two upper quadrant fascia panels together.
7 Put the upper quadrant fascia panels in position, and carefully move them behind the outer
ring cover on the gantry drum.
8 If necessary, use the washers as shims below the upper quadrant fascia panels to align
them with the covers. If there is space between the panels, make sure that it is the same at
the top and bottom.
9 If it is necessary, adjust the upper quadrant fascia panels until there is:
– An equal clearance at the bottom and the top of the panels
– A clearance of 4 mm between the gantry drum ring cover, and the lower quadrant
fascia panel.
10 Make sure that the front surfaces of the upper quadrant fascia panels and the gantry drum
covers align. If it is necessary, use the washers as shims to align them.
11 Use the two M8 × 65 mm screws to attach the upper quadrant fascia panels to the fascia
support post.
